hero.title: Prof. Dr. Ahmed Abdellatif
hero.subtitle: Professor & Consultant of Urology, Kidney Surgery, Endoscopy, and Andrology
hero.description: Specialized medical care under the supervision of top academic expertise, utilizing the latest global technologies in urology and endoscopy.

about.title: About Prof. Dr. Ahmed Abdellatif
about.description: Professor and Consultant of Urology and Venereology at the University Hospital, holding a PhD in Urology from top Egyptian and American universities. Dr. Ahmed possesses extensive experience in delicate surgeries and endoscopy, with a sub-specialization in andrology, infertility, and sexual health, committed to delivering the highest standards of medical care.

mission.title: Our Mission
mission.content: To provide outstanding medical care in urological surgery using the latest global technologies to ensure the health and comfort of our patients.

vision.title: Our Vision
vision.content: To be the leading and preferred center for urological surgery and andrology in Egypt and the Middle East.

faqs.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faqs.subtitle: Answers to the most common questions about urological health

items.q: What are the symptoms of prostate enlargement?
items.a: Symptoms include difficulty urinating, weak urine flow, and frequent need to urinate, especially at night.

items.q: Can kidney stones be treated without surgery?
items.a: Yes, we use laser techniques and flexible endoscopes to fragment stones without any surgical incision.

items.q: What are the latest treatments for male infertility?
items.a: We adopt the latest global protocols and microscopic surgeries to treat varicocele and improve sperm quality.

patientGuide.title: Patient Guide
patientGuide.subtitle: Important information before and after medical procedures

emergency.title: Emergency Cases
emergency.description: In case of severe bleeding, unbearable pain, or complete urinary retention after surgery, please immediately head to the nearest emergency hospital or contact us directly on the 24-hour emergency number.

prep: Fast for 6-8 hours before surgical procedures.
Bring all previous medical reports and X-rays.
Inform the doctor about all regular medications.
Shower thoroughly on the morning of the procedure.
postOp: Drink plenty of fluids (3-4 liters daily).
Avoid lifting heavy weights for two weeks.
Strictly adhere to prescribed medication schedules.
Light walking at home to stimulate circulation.
prevention: Maintain regular water intake throughout the day.
